fields.name: file_projects_locations_list
fields.description: Lists information about the supported locations for this service. This method can be called in two ways: * **List all public locations:** Use the path `GET /v1/locations`. * **List project-visible locations:** Use the path `GET /v1/projects/{project_id}/locations`. This may include public locations as well as private or other locations specifically visible to the project.

args.name: filter
args.description: A filter to narrow down results to a preferred subset. The filtering language accepts strings like `"displayName=tokyo"`, and is documented in more detail in [AIP-160](https://google.aip.dev/160).

args.name: pageSize
args.description: The maximum number of results to return. If not set, the service selects a default.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: A page token received from the `next_page_token` field in the response. Send that page token to receive the subsequent page.

args.name: extraLocationTypes
args.description: Optional. Do not use this field. It is unsupported and is ignored unless explicitly documented otherwise. This is primarily for internal usage.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_get
fields.description: Gets information about a location.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_operations_list
fields.description: Lists operations that match the specified filter in the request. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `UNIMPLEMENTED`.

args.name: returnPartialSuccess
args.description: When set to `true`, operations that are reachable are returned as normal, and those that are unreachable are returned in the ListOperationsResponse.unreachable field. This can only be `true` when reading across collections. For example, when `parent` is set to `"projects/example/locations/-"`. This field is not supported by default and will result in an `UNIMPLEMENTED` error if set unless explicitly documented otherwise in service or product specific documentation.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_operations_get
fields.description: Gets the latest state of a long-running operation. Clients can use this method to poll the operation result at intervals as recommended by the API service.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_list
fields.description: Lists all instances in a project for either a specified location or for all locations.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: The maximum number of items to return.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: The next_page_token value to use if there are additional results to retrieve for this list request.

args.name: orderBy
args.description: Sort results. Supported values are "name", "name desc" or "" (unsorted).

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_get
fields.description: Gets the details of a specific instance.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_snapshots_list
fields.description: Lists all snapshots in a project for either a specified location or for all locations.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: The maximum number of items to return.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: The next_page_token value to use if there are additional results to retrieve for this list request.

args.name: orderBy
args.description: Sort results. Supported values are "name", "name desc" or "" (unsorted).

args.name: returnPartialSuccess
args.description: Optional. If true, allow partial responses for multi-regional Aggregated List requests.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_snapshots_get
fields.description: Gets the details of a specific snapshot.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_backups_list
fields.description: Lists all backups in a project for either a specified location or for all locations.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: The maximum number of items to return.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: The next_page_token value to use if there are additional results to retrieve for this list request.

args.name: orderBy
args.description: Sort results. Supported values are "name", "name desc" or "" (unsorted).

fields.name: file_projects_locations_backups_get
fields.description: Gets the details of a specific backup.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: ListLocationsResponse
types.description: The response message for Locations.ListLocations.

fields.name: locations
fields.description: A list of locations that matches the specified filter in the request.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: The standard List next-page token.

types.name: Location
types.description: A resource that represents a Google Cloud location.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Resource name for the location, which may vary between implementations. For example: `"projects/example-project/locations/us-east1"`

fields.name: locationId
fields.description: The canonical id for this location. For example: `"us-east1"`.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: The friendly name for this location, typically a nearby city name. For example, "Tokyo".

fields.name: labels
fields.description: Cross-service attributes for the location. For example {"cloud.googleapis.com/region": "us-east1"}

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Service-specific metadata. For example the available capacity at the given location.

types.name: JSON
types.description: The `JSON` scalar type represents JSON values as specified by [ECMA-404](http://www.ecma-international.org/publications/files/ECMA-ST/ECMA-404.pdf).

types.name: ListOperationsResponse
types.description: The response message for Operations.ListOperations.

fields.name: operations
fields.description: A list of operations that matches the specified filter in the request.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: The standard List next-page token.

fields.name: unreachable
fields.description: Unordered list. Unreachable resources. Populated when the request sets `ListOperationsRequest.return_partial_success` and reads across collections. For example, when attempting to list all resources across all supported locations.

types.name: Operation
types.description: This resource represents a long-running operation that is the result of a network API call.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The server-assigned name, which is only unique within the same service that originally returns it. If you use the default HTTP mapping, the `name` should be a resource name ending with `operations/{unique_id}`.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Service-specific metadata associated with the operation. It typically contains progress information and common metadata such as create time. Some services might not provide such metadata. Any method that returns a long-running operation should document the metadata type, if any.

fields.name: done
fields.description: If the value is `false`, it means the operation is still in progress. If `true`, the operation is completed, and either `error` or `response` is available.

fields.name: response
fields.description: The normal, successful response of the operation. If the original method returns no data on success, such as `Delete`, the response is `google.protobuf.Empty`. If the original method is standard `Get`/`Create`/`Update`, the response should be the resource. For other methods, the response should have the type `XxxResponse`, where `Xxx` is the original method name. For example, if the original method name is `TakeSnapshot()`, the inferred response type is `TakeSnapshotResponse`.

types.name: Status
types.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

fields.name: code
fields.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

fields.name: message
fields.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

fields.name: details
fields.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

types.name: ListInstancesResponse
types.description: ListInstancesResponse is the result of ListInstancesRequest.

fields.name: instances
fields.description: A list of instances in the project for the specified location. If the `{location}` value in the request is "-", the response contains a list of instances from all locations. If any location is unreachable, the response will only return instances in reachable locations and the "unreachable" field will be populated with a list of unreachable locations.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: The token you can use to retrieve the next page of results. Not returned if there are no more results in the list.

fields.name: unreachable
fields.description: Unordered list. Locations that could not be reached.

types.name: Instance
types.description: A Filestore instance.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. The resource name of the instance, in the format `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}`.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the instance (2048 characters or less).

fields.name: statusMessage
fields.description: Output only. Additional information about the instance state, if available.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. The time when the instance was created.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: Resource labels to represent user provided metadata.

fields.name: fileShares
fields.description: File system shares on the instance. For this version, only a single file share is supported.

fields.name: networks
fields.description: VPC networks to which the instance is connected. For this version, only a single network is supported.

fields.name: etag
fields.description: Server-specified ETag for the instance resource to prevent simultaneous updates from overwriting each other.

fields.name: satisfiesPzs
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: satisfiesPzi
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: kmsKeyName
fields.description: KMS key name used for data encryption.

fields.name: suspensionReasons
fields.description: Output only. Field indicates all the reasons the instance is in "SUSPENDED" state.

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Optional. Input only. Immutable. Tag key-value pairs bound to this resource. Each key must be a namespaced name and each value a short name. Example: "123456789012/environment" : "production", "123456789013/costCenter" : "marketing" See the documentation for more information: - Namespaced name: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/tags/tags-creating-and-managing#retrieving_tag_key - Short name: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/tags/tags-creating-and-managing#retrieving_tag_value

fields.name: customPerformanceSupported
fields.description: Output only. Indicates whether this instance supports configuring its performance. If true, the user can configure the instance's performance by using the 'performance_config' field.

fields.name: deletionProtectionEnabled
fields.description: Optional. Indicates whether the instance is protected against deletion.

fields.name: deletionProtectionReason
fields.description: Optional. The reason for enabling deletion protection.

fields.name: minCapacityGb
fields.description: Output only. The minimum capacity of the instance in GB.

fields.name: maxCapacityGb
fields.description: Output only. The maximum capacity of the instance in GB.

fields.name: capacityStepSizeGb
fields.description: Output only. The incremental increase or decrease in capacity, designated in some number of GB.

types.name: query_file_projects_locations_instances_list_instances_items_state
types.description: Output only. The instance state.

types.name: query_file_projects_locations_instances_list_instances_items_tier
types.description: The service tier of the instance.

types.name: FileShareConfig
types.description: File share configuration for the instance.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Required. The name of the file share. Must use 1-16 characters for the basic service tier and 1-63 characters for all other service tiers. Must use lowercase letters, numbers, or underscores `[a-z0-9_]`. Must start with a letter. Immutable.

fields.name: capacityGb
fields.description: File share capacity in gigabytes (GB). Filestore defines 1 GB as 1024^3 bytes.

fields.name: sourceBackup
fields.description: The resource name of the backup, in the format `projects/{project_number}/locations/{location_id}/backups/{backup_id}`, that this file share has been restored from.

fields.name: sourceBackupdrBackup
fields.description: The resource name of the BackupDR backup, in the format `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location_id}/backupVaults/{backupvault_id}/dataSources/{datasource_id}/backups/{backup_id}`, TODO (b/443690479) - Remove visibility restrictions once the feature is ready

fields.name: nfsExportOptions
fields.description: Nfs Export Options. There is a limit of 10 export options per file share.

types.name: BigInt
types.description: The `BigInt` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values.

types.name: NfsExportOptions
types.description: NFS export options specifications.

fields.name: ipRanges
fields.description: List of either an IPv4 addresses in the format `{octet1}.{octet2}.{octet3}.{octet4}` or CIDR ranges in the format `{octet1}.{octet2}.{octet3}.{octet4}/{mask size}` which may mount the file share. Overlapping IP ranges are not allowed, both within and across NfsExportOptions. An error will be returned. The limit is 64 IP ranges/addresses for each FileShareConfig among all NfsExportOptions.

fields.name: network
fields.description: Optional. The source VPC network for ip_ranges. Required for instances using Private Service Connect, optional otherwise. If provided, must be the same network specified in the `NetworkConfig.network` field.

fields.name: anonUid
fields.description: An integer representing the anonymous user id with a default value of 65534. Anon_uid may only be set with squash_mode of ROOT_SQUASH. An error will be returned if this field is specified for other squash_mode settings.

fields.name: anonGid
fields.description: An integer representing the anonymous group id with a default value of 65534. Anon_gid may only be set with squash_mode of ROOT_SQUASH. An error will be returned if this field is specified for other squash_mode settings.

types.name: query_file_projects_locations_instances_list_instances_items_fileShares_items_nfsExportOptions_items_accessMode
types.description: Either READ_ONLY, for allowing only read requests on the exported directory, or READ_WRITE, for allowing both read and write requests. The default is READ_WRITE.

types.name: query_file_projects_locations_instances_list_instances_items_fileShares_items_nfsExportOptions_items_squashMode
types.description: Either NO_ROOT_SQUASH, for allowing root access on the exported directory, or ROOT_SQUASH, for not allowing root access. The default is NO_ROOT_SQUASH.

types.name: NetworkConfig
types.description: Network configuration for the instance.

fields.name: network
fields.description: The name of the Google Compute Engine [VPC network](https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/vpc) to which the instance is connected.

fields.name: modes
fields.description: Internet protocol versions for which the instance has IP addresses assigned.

fields.name: reservedIpRange
fields.description: Optional, reserved_ip_range can have one of the following two types of values. * CIDR range value when using DIRECT_PEERING connect mode. * [Allocated IP address range](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/ip-addresses/reserve-static-internal-ip-address) when using PRIVATE_SERVICE_ACCESS connect mode. When the name of an allocated IP address range is specified, it must be one of the ranges associated with the private service access connection. When specified as a direct CIDR value, it must be a /29 CIDR block for Basic tier, a /24 CIDR block for High Scale tier, or a /26 CIDR block for Enterprise tier in one of the [internal IP address ranges](https://www.arin.net/reference/research/statistics/address_filters/) that identifies the range of IP addresses reserved for this instance. For example, 10.0.0.0/29, 192.168.0.0/24 or 192.168.0.0/26, respectively. The range you specify can't overlap with either existing subnets or assigned IP address ranges for other Filestore instances in the selected VPC network.

fields.name: ipAddresses
fields.description: Output only. IPv4 addresses in the format `{octet1}.{octet2}.{octet3}.{octet4}` or IPv6 addresses in the format `{block1}:{block2}:{block3}:{block4}:{block5}:{block6}:{block7}:{block8}`.

types.name: query_file_projects_locations_instances_list_instances_items_networks_items_connectMode
types.description: The network connect mode of the Filestore instance. If not provided, the connect mode defaults to DIRECT_PEERING.

types.name: PscConfig
types.description: Private Service Connect configuration.

fields.name: endpointProject
fields.description: Optional. Consumer service project in which the Private Service Connect endpoint would be set up. This is optional, and only relevant in case the network is a shared VPC. If this is not specified, the endpoint would be setup in the VPC host project.

types.name: Replication
types.description: Optional. The configuration used to replicate an instance.

fields.name: replicas
fields.description: Optional. Replication configuration for the replica instance associated with this instance. Only a single replica is supported.

types.name: query_file_projects_locations_instances_list_instances_items_replication_role
types.description: Optional. The replication role. When creating a new replica, this field must be set to `STANDBY`.

types.name: ReplicaConfig
types.description: Replica configuration for the instance.

fields.name: stateUpdateTime
fields.description: Output only. The time when the replica state was updated.

fields.name: stateReasons
fields.description: Output only. Additional information about the replication state, if available.

fields.name: peerInstance
fields.description: Optional. The name of the source instance for the replica, in the format `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}`. This field is required when creating a replica.

fields.name: lastActiveSyncTime
fields.description: Output only. The timestamp of the latest replication snapshot taken on the active instance and is already replicated safely.

types.name: query_file_projects_locations_instances_list_instances_items_replication_replicas_items_state
types.description: Output only. The replica state.

types.name: query_file_projects_locations_instances_list_instances_items_protocol
types.description: Immutable. The protocol indicates the access protocol for all shares in the instance. This field is immutable and it cannot be changed after the instance has been created. Default value: `NFS_V3`.

types.name: DirectoryServicesConfig
types.description: Directory Services configuration for Kerberos-based authentication.

types.name: LdapConfig
types.description: LdapConfig contains all the parameters for connecting to LDAP servers.

fields.name: domain
fields.description: Required. The LDAP domain name in the format of `my-domain.com`.

fields.name: servers
fields.description: Required. The servers names are used for specifying the LDAP servers names. The LDAP servers names can come with two formats: 1. DNS name, for example: `ldap.example1.com`, `ldap.example2.com`. 2. IP address, for example: `10.0.0.1`, `10.0.0.2`, `10.0.0.3`. All servers names must be in the same format: either all DNS names or all IP addresses.

fields.name: usersOu
fields.description: Optional. The users Organizational Unit (OU) is optional. This parameter is a hint to allow faster lookup in the LDAP namespace. In case that this parameter is not provided, Filestore instance will query the whole LDAP namespace.

fields.name: groupsOu
fields.description: Optional. The groups Organizational Unit (OU) is optional. This parameter is a hint to allow faster lookup in the LDAP namespace. In case that this parameter is not provided, Filestore instance will query the whole LDAP namespace.

types.name: PerformanceConfig
types.description: Used for setting the performance configuration. If the user doesn't specify PerformanceConfig, automatically provision the default performance settings as described in https://cloud.google.com/filestore/docs/performance. Larger instances will be linearly set to more IOPS. If the instance's capacity is increased or decreased, its performance will be automatically adjusted upwards or downwards accordingly (respectively).

types.name: IOPSPerTB
types.description: IOPS per TB. Filestore defines TB as 1024^4 bytes (TiB).

fields.name: maxIopsPerTb
fields.description: Required. Maximum IOPS per TiB.

types.name: FixedIOPS
types.description: Fixed IOPS (input/output operations per second) parameters.

fields.name: maxIops
fields.description: Required. Maximum IOPS.

types.name: PerformanceLimits
types.description: The enforced performance limits, calculated from the instance's performance configuration.

fields.name: maxIops
fields.description: Output only. The maximum IOPS.

fields.name: maxReadIops
fields.description: Output only. The maximum read IOPS.

fields.name: maxWriteIops
fields.description: Output only. The maximum write IOPS.

fields.name: maxReadThroughputBps
fields.description: Output only. The maximum read throughput in bytes per second.

fields.name: maxWriteThroughputBps
fields.description: Output only. The maximum write throughput in bytes per second.

types.name: ListSnapshotsResponse
types.description: ListSnapshotsResponse is the result of ListSnapshotsRequest.

fields.name: snapshots
fields.description: A list of snapshots in the project for the specified instance.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: The token you can use to retrieve the next page of results. Not returned if there are no more results in the list.

fields.name: unreachable
fields.description: Unordered list. Locations that could not be reached.

types.name: Snapshot
types.description: A Filestore snapshot.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. The resource name of the snapshot, in the format `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location_id}/instances/{instance_id}/snapshots/{snapshot_id}`.

fields.name: description
fields.description: A description of the snapshot with 2048 characters or less. Requests with longer descriptions will be rejected.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. The time when the snapshot was created.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: Resource labels to represent user provided metadata.

fields.name: filesystemUsedBytes
fields.description: Output only. The amount of bytes needed to allocate a full copy of the snapshot content

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Optional. Input only. Immutable. Tag key-value pairs bound to this resource. Each key must be a namespaced name and each value a short name. Example: "123456789012/environment" : "production", "123456789013/costCenter" : "marketing" See the documentation for more information: - Namespaced name: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/tags/tags-creating-and-managing#retrieving_tag_key - Short name: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/tags/tags-creating-and-managing#retrieving_tag_value

types.name: query_file_projects_locations_instances_snapshots_list_snapshots_items_state
types.description: Output only. The snapshot state.

types.name: ListBackupsResponse
types.description: ListBackupsResponse is the result of ListBackupsRequest.

fields.name: backups
fields.description: A list of backups in the project for the specified location. If the `{location}` value in the request is "-", the response contains a list of backups from all locations. If any location is unreachable, the response will only return backups in reachable locations and the "unreachable" field will be populated with a list of unreachable locations.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: The token you can use to retrieve the next page of results. Not returned if there are no more results in the list.

fields.name: unreachable
fields.description: Unordered list. Locations that could not be reached.

types.name: Backup
types.description: A Filestore backup.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. The resource name of the backup, in the format `projects/{project_number}/locations/{location_id}/backups/{backup_id}`.

fields.name: description
fields.description: A description of the backup with 2048 characters or less. Requests with longer descriptions will be rejected.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. The time when the backup was created.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: Resource labels to represent user provided metadata.

fields.name: capacityGb
fields.description: Output only. Capacity of the source file share when the backup was created.

fields.name: storageBytes
fields.description: Output only. The size of the storage used by the backup. As backups share storage, this number is expected to change with backup creation/deletion.

fields.name: sourceInstance
fields.description: The resource name of the source Filestore instance, in the format `projects/{project_number}/locations/{location_id}/instances/{instance_id}`, used to create this backup.

fields.name: sourceFileShare
fields.description: Name of the file share in the source Filestore instance that the backup is created from.

fields.name: downloadBytes
fields.description: Output only. Amount of bytes that will be downloaded if the backup is restored. This may be different than storage bytes, since sequential backups of the same disk will share storage.

fields.name: satisfiesPzs
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: satisfiesPzi
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: kmsKey
fields.description: Immutable. KMS key name used for data encryption.

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Optional. Input only. Immutable. Tag key-value pairs bound to this resource. Each key must be a namespaced name and each value a short name. Example: "123456789012/environment" : "production", "123456789013/costCenter" : "marketing" See the documentation for more information: - Namespaced name: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/tags/tags-creating-and-managing#retrieving_tag_key - Short name: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/tags/tags-creating-and-managing#retrieving_tag_value

types.name: query_file_projects_locations_backups_list_backups_items_state
types.description: Output only. The backup state.

types.name: query_file_projects_locations_backups_list_backups_items_sourceInstanceTier
types.description: Output only. The service tier of the source Filestore instance that this backup is created from.

types.name: query_file_projects_locations_backups_list_backups_items_fileSystemProtocol
types.description: Output only. The file system protocol of the source Filestore instance that this backup is created from.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_operations_delete
fields.description: Deletes a long-running operation. This method indicates that the client is no longer interested in the operation result. It does not cancel the operation. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_operations_cancel
fields.description: Starts asynchronous cancellation on a long-running operation. The server makes a best effort to cancel the operation, but success is not guaranteed. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`. Clients can use Operations.GetOperation or other methods to check whether the cancellation succeeded or whether the operation completed despite cancellation. On successful cancellation, the operation is not deleted; instead, it becomes an operation with an Operation.error value with a google.rpc.Status.code of `1`, corresponding to `Code.CANCELLED`.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_create
fields.description: Creates an instance. When creating from a backup, the capacity of the new instance needs to be equal to or larger than the capacity of the backup (and also equal to or larger than the minimum capacity of the tier).

args.name: instanceId
args.description: Required. The name of the instance to create. The name must be unique for the specified project and location.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_patch
fields.description: Updates the settings of a specific instance.

args.name: updateMask
args.description: Mask of fields to update. At least one path must be supplied in this field. The elements of the repeated paths field may only include these fields: * "description" * "file_shares" * "labels" * "performance_config" * "deletion_protection_enabled" * "deletion_protection_reason"

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_delete
fields.description: Deletes an instance.

args.name: force
args.description: If set to true, all snapshots of the instance will also be deleted. (Otherwise, the request will only work if the instance has no snapshots.)

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_restore
fields.description: Restores an existing instance's file share from a backup. The capacity of the instance needs to be equal to or larger than the capacity of the backup (and also equal to or larger than the minimum capacity of the tier).

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_revert
fields.description: Revert an existing instance's file system to a specified snapshot.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_promoteReplica
fields.description: Promote the standby instance (replica).

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_pauseReplica
fields.description: Pause the standby instance (replica). WARNING: This operation makes the standby instance's NFS filesystem writable. Any data written to the standby instance while paused will be lost when the replica is resumed or promoted.

args.name: input
args.description: PauseReplicaRequest pauses a Filestore standby instance (replica).

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_resumeReplica
fields.description: Resume the standby instance (replica). WARNING: Any data written to the standby instance while paused will be lost when the replica is resumed.

args.name: input
args.description: ResumeReplicaRequest resumes a Filestore standby instance (replica).

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_snapshots_create
fields.description: Creates a snapshot.

args.name: snapshotId
args.description: Required. The ID to use for the snapshot. The ID must be unique within the specified instance. This value must start with a lowercase letter followed by up to 62 lowercase letters, numbers, or hyphens, and cannot end with a hyphen.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_snapshots_delete
fields.description: Deletes a snapshot.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_instances_snapshots_patch
fields.description: Updates the settings of a specific snapshot.

args.name: updateMask
args.description: Required. Mask of fields to update. At least one path must be supplied in this field.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_backups_create
fields.description: Creates a backup.

args.name: backupId
args.description: Required. The ID to use for the backup. The ID must be unique within the specified project and location. This value must start with a lowercase letter followed by up to 62 lowercase letters, numbers, or hyphens, and cannot end with a hyphen. Values that do not match this pattern will trigger an INVALID_ARGUMENT error.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_backups_delete
fields.description: Deletes a backup.

fields.name: file_projects_locations_backups_patch
fields.description: Updates the settings of a specific backup.

args.name: updateMask
args.description: Required. Mask of fields to update. At least one path must be supplied in this field.

types.name: Instance_Input
types.description: A Filestore instance.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Output only. The resource name of the instance, in the format `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}`.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of the instance (2048 characters or less).

inputFields.name: statusMessage
inputFields.description: Output only. Additional information about the instance state, if available.

inputFields.name: createTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time when the instance was created.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: Resource labels to represent user provided metadata.

inputFields.name: fileShares
inputFields.description: File system shares on the instance. For this version, only a single file share is supported.

inputFields.name: networks
inputFields.description: VPC networks to which the instance is connected. For this version, only a single network is supported.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: Server-specified ETag for the instance resource to prevent simultaneous updates from overwriting each other.

inputFields.name: satisfiesPzs
inputFields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

inputFields.name: satisfiesPzi
inputFields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

inputFields.name: kmsKeyName
inputFields.description: KMS key name used for data encryption.

inputFields.name: suspensionReasons
inputFields.description: Output only. Field indicates all the reasons the instance is in "SUSPENDED" state.

inputFields.name: tags
inputFields.description: Optional. Input only. Immutable. Tag key-value pairs bound to this resource. Each key must be a namespaced name and each value a short name. Example: "123456789012/environment" : "production", "123456789013/costCenter" : "marketing" See the documentation for more information: - Namespaced name: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/tags/tags-creating-and-managing#retrieving_tag_key - Short name: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/tags/tags-creating-and-managing#retrieving_tag_value

inputFields.name: customPerformanceSupported
inputFields.description: Output only. Indicates whether this instance supports configuring its performance. If true, the user can configure the instance's performance by using the 'performance_config' field.

inputFields.name: deletionProtectionEnabled
inputFields.description: Optional. Indicates whether the instance is protected against deletion.

inputFields.name: deletionProtectionReason
inputFields.description: Optional. The reason for enabling deletion protection.

inputFields.name: minCapacityGb
inputFields.description: Output only. The minimum capacity of the instance in GB.

inputFields.name: maxCapacityGb
inputFields.description: Output only. The maximum capacity of the instance in GB.

inputFields.name: capacityStepSizeGb
inputFields.description: Output only. The incremental increase or decrease in capacity, designated in some number of GB.

types.name: FileShareConfig_Input
types.description: File share configuration for the instance.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Required. The name of the file share. Must use 1-16 characters for the basic service tier and 1-63 characters for all other service tiers. Must use lowercase letters, numbers, or underscores `[a-z0-9_]`. Must start with a letter. Immutable.

inputFields.name: capacityGb
inputFields.description: File share capacity in gigabytes (GB). Filestore defines 1 GB as 1024^3 bytes.

inputFields.name: sourceBackup
inputFields.description: The resource name of the backup, in the format `projects/{project_number}/locations/{location_id}/backups/{backup_id}`, that this file share has been restored from.

inputFields.name: sourceBackupdrBackup
inputFields.description: The resource name of the BackupDR backup, in the format `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location_id}/backupVaults/{backupvault_id}/dataSources/{datasource_id}/backups/{backup_id}`, TODO (b/443690479) - Remove visibility restrictions once the feature is ready

inputFields.name: nfsExportOptions
inputFields.description: Nfs Export Options. There is a limit of 10 export options per file share.

types.name: NfsExportOptions_Input
types.description: NFS export options specifications.

inputFields.name: ipRanges
inputFields.description: List of either an IPv4 addresses in the format `{octet1}.{octet2}.{octet3}.{octet4}` or CIDR ranges in the format `{octet1}.{octet2}.{octet3}.{octet4}/{mask size}` which may mount the file share. Overlapping IP ranges are not allowed, both within and across NfsExportOptions. An error will be returned. The limit is 64 IP ranges/addresses for each FileShareConfig among all NfsExportOptions.

inputFields.name: network
inputFields.description: Optional. The source VPC network for ip_ranges. Required for instances using Private Service Connect, optional otherwise. If provided, must be the same network specified in the `NetworkConfig.network` field.

inputFields.name: anonUid
inputFields.description: An integer representing the anonymous user id with a default value of 65534. Anon_uid may only be set with squash_mode of ROOT_SQUASH. An error will be returned if this field is specified for other squash_mode settings.

inputFields.name: anonGid
inputFields.description: An integer representing the anonymous group id with a default value of 65534. Anon_gid may only be set with squash_mode of ROOT_SQUASH. An error will be returned if this field is specified for other squash_mode settings.

types.name: NetworkConfig_Input
types.description: Network configuration for the instance.

inputFields.name: network
inputFields.description: The name of the Google Compute Engine [VPC network](https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/vpc) to which the instance is connected.

inputFields.name: modes
inputFields.description: Internet protocol versions for which the instance has IP addresses assigned.

inputFields.name: reservedIpRange
inputFields.description: Optional, reserved_ip_range can have one of the following two types of values. * CIDR range value when using DIRECT_PEERING connect mode. * [Allocated IP address range](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/ip-addresses/reserve-static-internal-ip-address) when using PRIVATE_SERVICE_ACCESS connect mode. When the name of an allocated IP address range is specified, it must be one of the ranges associated with the private service access connection. When specified as a direct CIDR value, it must be a /29 CIDR block for Basic tier, a /24 CIDR block for High Scale tier, or a /26 CIDR block for Enterprise tier in one of the [internal IP address ranges](https://www.arin.net/reference/research/statistics/address_filters/) that identifies the range of IP addresses reserved for this instance. For example, 10.0.0.0/29, 192.168.0.0/24 or 192.168.0.0/26, respectively. The range you specify can't overlap with either existing subnets or assigned IP address ranges for other Filestore instances in the selected VPC network.

inputFields.name: ipAddresses
inputFields.description: Output only. IPv4 addresses in the format `{octet1}.{octet2}.{octet3}.{octet4}` or IPv6 addresses in the format `{block1}:{block2}:{block3}:{block4}:{block5}:{block6}:{block7}:{block8}`.

types.name: PscConfig_Input
types.description: Private Service Connect configuration.

inputFields.name: endpointProject
inputFields.description: Optional. Consumer service project in which the Private Service Connect endpoint would be set up. This is optional, and only relevant in case the network is a shared VPC. If this is not specified, the endpoint would be setup in the VPC host project.

types.name: Replication_Input
types.description: Optional. The configuration used to replicate an instance.

inputFields.name: replicas
inputFields.description: Optional. Replication configuration for the replica instance associated with this instance. Only a single replica is supported.

types.name: ReplicaConfig_Input
types.description: Replica configuration for the instance.

inputFields.name: stateUpdateTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time when the replica state was updated.

inputFields.name: stateReasons
inputFields.description: Output only. Additional information about the replication state, if available.

inputFields.name: peerInstance
inputFields.description: Optional. The name of the source instance for the replica, in the format `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}`. This field is required when creating a replica.

inputFields.name: lastActiveSyncTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The timestamp of the latest replication snapshot taken on the active instance and is already replicated safely.

types.name: DirectoryServicesConfig_Input
types.description: Directory Services configuration for Kerberos-based authentication.

types.name: LdapConfig_Input
types.description: LdapConfig contains all the parameters for connecting to LDAP servers.

inputFields.name: domain
inputFields.description: Required. The LDAP domain name in the format of `my-domain.com`.

inputFields.name: servers
inputFields.description: Required. The servers names are used for specifying the LDAP servers names. The LDAP servers names can come with two formats: 1. DNS name, for example: `ldap.example1.com`, `ldap.example2.com`. 2. IP address, for example: `10.0.0.1`, `10.0.0.2`, `10.0.0.3`. All servers names must be in the same format: either all DNS names or all IP addresses.

inputFields.name: usersOu
inputFields.description: Optional. The users Organizational Unit (OU) is optional. This parameter is a hint to allow faster lookup in the LDAP namespace. In case that this parameter is not provided, Filestore instance will query the whole LDAP namespace.

inputFields.name: groupsOu
inputFields.description: Optional. The groups Organizational Unit (OU) is optional. This parameter is a hint to allow faster lookup in the LDAP namespace. In case that this parameter is not provided, Filestore instance will query the whole LDAP namespace.

types.name: PerformanceConfig_Input
types.description: Used for setting the performance configuration. If the user doesn't specify PerformanceConfig, automatically provision the default performance settings as described in https://cloud.google.com/filestore/docs/performance. Larger instances will be linearly set to more IOPS. If the instance's capacity is increased or decreased, its performance will be automatically adjusted upwards or downwards accordingly (respectively).

types.name: IOPSPerTB_Input
types.description: IOPS per TB. Filestore defines TB as 1024^4 bytes (TiB).

inputFields.name: maxIopsPerTb
inputFields.description: Required. Maximum IOPS per TiB.

types.name: FixedIOPS_Input
types.description: Fixed IOPS (input/output operations per second) parameters.

inputFields.name: maxIops
inputFields.description: Required. Maximum IOPS.

types.name: PerformanceLimits_Input
types.description: The enforced performance limits, calculated from the instance's performance configuration.

inputFields.name: maxIops
inputFields.description: Output only. The maximum IOPS.

inputFields.name: maxReadIops
inputFields.description: Output only. The maximum read IOPS.

inputFields.name: maxWriteIops
inputFields.description: Output only. The maximum write IOPS.

inputFields.name: maxReadThroughputBps
inputFields.description: Output only. The maximum read throughput in bytes per second.

inputFields.name: maxWriteThroughputBps
inputFields.description: Output only. The maximum write throughput in bytes per second.

types.name: RestoreInstanceRequest_Input
types.description: RestoreInstanceRequest restores an existing instance's file share from a backup.

inputFields.name: fileShare
inputFields.description: Required. Name of the file share in the Filestore instance that the backup is being restored to.

inputFields.name: sourceBackup
inputFields.description: The resource name of the backup, in the format `projects/{project_number}/locations/{location_id}/backups/{backup_id}`.

types.name: RevertInstanceRequest_Input
types.description: RevertInstanceRequest reverts the given instance's file share to the specified snapshot.

inputFields.name: targetSnapshotId
inputFields.description: Required. The snapshot resource ID, in the format 'my-snapshot', where the specified ID is the {snapshot_id} of the fully qualified name like `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location_id}/instances/{instance_id}/snapshots/{snapshot_id}`

types.name: PromoteReplicaRequest_Input
types.description: PromoteReplicaRequest promotes a Filestore standby instance (replica).

inputFields.name: peerInstance
inputFields.description: Optional. The resource name of the peer instance to promote, in the format `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location_id}/instances/{instance_id}`. The peer instance is required if the operation is called on an active instance.

types.name: Snapshot_Input
types.description: A Filestore snapshot.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Output only. The resource name of the snapshot, in the format `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location_id}/instances/{instance_id}/snapshots/{snapshot_id}`.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: A description of the snapshot with 2048 characters or less. Requests with longer descriptions will be rejected.

inputFields.name: createTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time when the snapshot was created.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: Resource labels to represent user provided metadata.

inputFields.name: filesystemUsedBytes
inputFields.description: Output only. The amount of bytes needed to allocate a full copy of the snapshot content

inputFields.name: tags
inputFields.description: Optional. Input only. Immutable. Tag key-value pairs bound to this resource. Each key must be a namespaced name and each value a short name. Example: "123456789012/environment" : "production", "123456789013/costCenter" : "marketing" See the documentation for more information: - Namespaced name: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/tags/tags-creating-and-managing#retrieving_tag_key - Short name: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/tags/tags-creating-and-managing#retrieving_tag_value

types.name: Backup_Input
types.description: A Filestore backup.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Output only. The resource name of the backup, in the format `projects/{project_number}/locations/{location_id}/backups/{backup_id}`.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: A description of the backup with 2048 characters or less. Requests with longer descriptions will be rejected.

inputFields.name: createTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time when the backup was created.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: Resource labels to represent user provided metadata.

inputFields.name: capacityGb
inputFields.description: Output only. Capacity of the source file share when the backup was created.

inputFields.name: storageBytes
inputFields.description: Output only. The size of the storage used by the backup. As backups share storage, this number is expected to change with backup creation/deletion.

inputFields.name: sourceInstance
inputFields.description: The resource name of the source Filestore instance, in the format `projects/{project_number}/locations/{location_id}/instances/{instance_id}`, used to create this backup.

inputFields.name: sourceFileShare
inputFields.description: Name of the file share in the source Filestore instance that the backup is created from.

inputFields.name: downloadBytes
inputFields.description: Output only. Amount of bytes that will be downloaded if the backup is restored. This may be different than storage bytes, since sequential backups of the same disk will share storage.

inputFields.name: satisfiesPzs
inputFields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

inputFields.name: satisfiesPzi
inputFields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

inputFields.name: kmsKey
inputFields.description: Immutable. KMS key name used for data encryption.

inputFields.name: tags
inputFields.description: Optional. Input only. Immutable. Tag key-value pairs bound to this resource. Each key must be a namespaced name and each value a short name. Example: "123456789012/environment" : "production", "123456789013/costCenter" : "marketing" See the documentation for more information: - Namespaced name: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/tags/tags-creating-and-managing#retrieving_tag_key - Short name: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/tags/tags-creating-and-managing#retrieving_tag_value

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
